info.title: CodeSearch Management API
info.description:
HTTP management API for the `codesearch serve` command. Exposes codesearch operations over REST/JSON and Server-Sent Events (SSE), separate from the MCP protocol server.

This document is checked in at `docs/management-api.openapi.json` and served verbatim at `GET /api/openapi.json`.

## Errors

Every endpoint returns errors as `{ "error": "<message>" }` with an appropriate status: `400` for malformed input (unknown protocol / memory kind, bad body), `404` when a named repository, symbol, or memory item is not found, and `500` for any other use-case failure.

## REST endpoints

Paths under `/api/...` (excluding `/api/stream/...`) are request/response JSON. Query-string filters are documented per path.

## Streaming (SSE) endpoints

Paths under `/api/stream/` respond with `text/event-stream`. Each frame is a named SSE event (`event:`) with a JSON `data:` payload. A terminal `done` or `error` event is always the last frame; clients treat either as end-of-stream. If the client disconnects, the server drops the in-flight work. Because OpenAPI cannot express SSE frame shapes natively, the `event` names and payloads are mirrored in the `SseEvent*` component schemas.

## Domain response shapes

Several endpoints return a domain value serialized directly (impact analysis, symbol context, cluster graphs, the channel-link report, memory items). Their documented top-level fields are precise; nested objects are marked `additionalProperties: true` and reference the owning Rust domain type, since pinning every nested field here would be brittle against domain refactors.

servers.description: Local management server (default bind). Use 0.0.0.0 when started with --public.

tags.name: graph
tags.description: Call-graph queries: impact, context, cross-repo uses, execution features.

tags.name: memory
tags.description: Long-term memory queries and dream-cycle management.

get.description: Self-describing document listing the currently available endpoints. Also served at `/`.

200.description: Every repository indexed in the current namespace.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Repository name or UUID.

get.summary: Get one repository plus its architecture overview
get.description: Returns the repository fields plus `architecture_overview`, a best-effort Markdown cluster summary (empty string when the repository has no call graph).

architecture_overview.description: Markdown cluster summary; empty when unavailable.

delete.description: Deletes by ID, falling back to deletion by path when the ID is not found.

404.description: No repository matches the id or path.

200.description: Aggregate counts across all repositories in the namespace.

post.summary: Blast radius of changing a symbol
post.description: BFS up the call graph from the given symbol. Returns the serialized `ImpactAnalysis` domain value.

404.description: The symbol was not found in the call graph.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: Symbol name, or regex when `regex=true`.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: Restrict to a repository (name or UUID).

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: Interpret `{symbol}` as a regular expression.

get.summary: Callers and callees of a symbol
get.description: Returns the serialized `SymbolContext` domain value (callers up to entry points, callees down to leaves).
Files in `from` that reference symbols defined in `to`.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Repository doing the using (name or UUID).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Repository being used (name or UUID).

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: Repository to analyse (name or UUID). Omit to auto-detect.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of features to return.

get.description: Architectural clusters over the file dependency graph. Returns the serialized `ClusterGraph` domain value.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: Repository to analyse (name or UUID). Omit to auto-detect.

get.description: Leiden communities over the symbol call graph. Returns the serialized `SymbolCommunityGraph` domain value.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: Repository to analyse (name or UUID). Omit to auto-detect.

get.summary: Coupling elements holding fragile communities together
get.description: Files/symbols or dependencies whose removal would split a Leiden community into two latent sub-blocks (the hub-like dependency / modularity-violation smell). Returns the serialized `CouplingReport` domain value.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: Repository to analyse (name or UUID). Omit to auto-detect.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Which graph to analyse: the file-dependency graph or the symbol call graph.

get.description: Matched producer→consumer links (Kafka topics, HTTP routes, MQTT/AMQP topics, gRPC methods). Returns the serialized `ChannelLinkReport` domain value. With no `repository`, scoped to the current namespace's repositories.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: Restrict to specific repositories (name or UUID). Repeatable.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description: Filter by protocol.

parameters.name: min_confidence
parameters.description: Drop edges below this confidence (0.0–1.0).

parameters.name: exclude_channel
parameters.description: Exclude channels matching these globs. Repeatable.

parameters.name: include_tests
parameters.description: Include endpoints from test files (excluded by default).

404.description: A named repository was not found.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Restrict to one memory kind.

get.description: Hybrid semantic + keyword search. Each result carries a `score` alongside the memory-item fields.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query.

parameters.name: num
parameters.description: Maximum number of results.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Restrict to one memory kind.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Restrict to memories relevant in this project/namespace (its items plus globals). Omit to search every project.

200.description: Counts of stored items and imported sessions.

get.description: With no `uri`, returns the digest node plus the sessions and resources directories. With a `uri`, returns that directory's children.

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: Directory URI to list (e.g. `memory://sessions`).

get.description: Scheduler configuration (from the `memory` section of `config.json`), whether a cycle is currently running, and the last recorded run. Returns `503` when the server started without a usable LLM backend.

503.description: Dreaming is not available on this server.

post.description: Starts a dream cycle (harvest finished sessions, consolidate near-duplicate/contradictory memories, reflect, refresh the digest) in the background and returns immediately. Poll `GET /api/memory/dream` for the recorded outcome.

409.description: A dream cycle is already running.

503.description: Dreaming is not available on this server.

put.description: Update the dream scheduler's settings. Accepts a partial body; omitted fields are unchanged. Persisted to config.json and applied to the running scheduler live (no restart). Returns the merged effective config.

503.description: Dreaming unavailable (no LLM backend at startup)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A memory item UUID, a `kind/name` reference, or a `memory://…` node URI.

get.summary: Get one memory item or virtual-filesystem node
get.description: Returns `{ "item": MemoryItem }` for an item address, or `{ "node": MemoryNode }` when the id is a `memory://` URI.

404.description: No item or node at the given address.

get.description: Finished assistant sessions (Claude Code / OpenCode / Zed) found on this machine, newest first. Each entry carries display fields; the import-status map (GET /api/sessions/import) says which are already imported.
Full per-turn transcript of a discovered session, for a preview before importing.
Import status of every tracked session, keyed by (source, id): already_imported, queued, importing, done, or failed.

post.description: Queue a background import of one discovered session. Returns 202 immediately; the import runs on a detached task and progress is polled via GET /api/sessions/import.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: The symbol whose call flow to explain. Interpreted as a regex when the request body sets `regex: true`.

get.description:
Streams the LLM's explanation of a symbol's bidirectional call flow token by token.

**Response is `text/event-stream`.** Events, in order:
- `token` — one chunk of the explanation; payload `{ "text": string }`. Zero or more.
- `done` — terminal summary; payload `SseExplainDone`. Exactly one on success.
- `error` — terminal failure; payload `{ "message": string }`. Replaces `done` on failure.

If the symbol is ambiguous (matches several fully-qualified names), no `token` events are emitted and the `done` event carries `status: "ambiguous"` with a `candidates` list instead of an explanation.

No request body is required for `GET`; use `POST` to pass options.

200.description: SSE stream of `token` events followed by a terminal `done` or `error` event.

post.description: Same as the `GET` form, but accepts an optional JSON body of options.

200.description: SSE stream of `token` events followed by a terminal `done` or `error` event.

post.description:
Indexes a repository path and streams coarse lifecycle progress.

**Response is `text/event-stream`.** Events, in order:
- `progress` — a lifecycle update; payload `{ "stage": string, "message": string }`. `stage` is `started` first, then periodic `running` heartbeats.
- `done` — terminal summary; payload `SseIndexDone`. Exactly one on success.
- `error` — terminal failure; payload `{ "message": string }`. Replaces `done` on failure.

Progress is coarse (start / running heartbeats / done) because the underlying use case does not yet expose a per-file progress channel.

200.description: SSE stream of `progress` events followed by a terminal `done` or `error` event.

Error.description: Uniform error body returned with a 4xx/5xx status.

version.description: Crate version of the running server.

languages.description: Map of language name → per-language stats.

query.description: Natural-language query describing what the code does.

repository.description: Restrict to a repository (name or UUID).

ImpactAnalysis.description: Serialized `ImpactAnalysis` domain value: the blast radius of changing a symbol. Nested affected-symbol entries carry the symbol name, repository, file, line, and depth.

SymbolContext.description: Serialized `SymbolContext` domain value: callers (entry points → symbol) and callees (symbol → leaves) as trees.

FileEdge.description: Serialized `FileEdge` domain value: one cross-file dependency edge (source/target files and repositories, reference details).

ExecutionFeature.description: Serialized execution-feature domain value: an entry point with its criticality score and contributing factors.

ClusterGraph.description: Serialized `ClusterGraph` domain value: Leiden clusters over the file dependency graph (clusters, members, cohesion, inter-cluster edges).

SymbolCommunityGraph.description: Serialized `SymbolCommunityGraph` domain value: Leiden communities over the symbol call graph.

CouplingReport.description: Serialized `CouplingReport` domain value: per internally-fragile Leiden community, its two latent sub-blocks and the ablation-verified coupling elements (nodes/edges) holding them together, with split probabilities and the resolution range each controls.

ChannelLinkReport.description: Serialized `ChannelLinkReport` domain value: matched producer→consumer channel links with protocol, channel name, endpoints, and confidence.

MemoryItem.description: Serialized memory item: a durable fact/preference/experience/skill extracted from a session.

MemoryNode.description: Serialized memory virtual-filesystem node (digest, session, or resource directory/leaf).

ExplainStreamRequest.description: Optional options for the explain stream. All fields optional.

repository.description: Restrict the explanation to a single repository (name or UUID).

regex.description: Interpret the `{symbol}` path segment as a regular expression.

llm.description: LLM backend to use. Defaults to the server's configured LLM backend.

path.description: Filesystem path of the repository to index.

name.description: Optional human-readable name (defaults to the directory name).

force.description: Force a full re-index, discarding existing data for this path.

SseExplainEvent.description: One SSE frame emitted by `/api/stream/explain/{symbol}`. `event` names the frame; `data` is the JSON payload. Modelled as a union for documentation — the wire format is SSE, not JSON.

SseTokenEvent.description: `event: token` — one chunk of streamed explanation text.

SseErrorEvent.description: `event: error` — terminal failure. Last frame of the stream.

SseExplainDoneEvent.description: `event: done` — terminal success for the explain stream.

explanation.description: Full post-processed Markdown explanation (also delivered incrementally as `token` events).

SseIndexDoneEvent.description: `event: done` — terminal success for the index stream.

DreamRun.description: Record of one completed dream (memory consolidation) cycle.

status.description: Cycle outcome: `completed`, or `failed: <reason>` when a phase errored after earlier phases may have already written.
